perturbation.original_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara, Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Francisco and San Mateo located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara and Alameda located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the originally defined Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the Restricted Territory in Section 3(a) to include only Santa Clara and Alameda counties, while actions are still prohibited anywhere in the 'originally defined' Restricted Territory within the same paragraph. This creates ambiguity regarding the actual scope of the non-compete agreement, as the definition of Restricted Territory now varies within the same clause, contradicting the restriction on actions elsewhere in the original, broader territory. This creates uncertainity regarding enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder hereby covenants and agrees that from the Effective Date until the third (3 rd ) anniversary of the Effective Date (“Restricted Period”), Shareholder will not without the prior written consent of Heritage, engage or participate or have any interest, directly or indirectly, in any Business anywhere in the counties of Santa Clara and Alameda located in the State of California (“Restricted Territory”) (all such entities shall be referred to each as “Competitor” or collectively as “Competitors,”), with respect to the following acts: (i) own any equity interest in any Competitor; (ii) operate, join, control, advise, become a founder or otherwise participate in any Competitor; (iii) lend credit or money for the purpose of assisting another to establish or operate any Competitor; (iv) request or advise any customer, strategic partner or vendor of Diablo that becomes a present or future customer, strategic partner or vendor of Heritage, HBC or their subsidiaries now and hereinafter existing (collectively, the “ Affiliated Companies ”) to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business with Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies anywhere in the originally defined Restricted Territory; (v) induce or influence (or attempt to induce or influence) any person or entity who is engaged (as an employee, agent, independent contractor or otherwise) by Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to terminate his, her or its employment or engagement for the purpose of obtaining employment with a Competitor; (vi) solicit any employee of Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies to leave employment and become affiliated with any Competitor; (vii) solicit any actual or “prospective customer” (as hereinafter defined), which was served by Diablo in connection with any business of Diablo, or (viii) solicit, influence or attempt to influence any customer which is or was served by Diablo to discontinue its business or service available from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies; provided , that , Shareholder may own and hold as an investment of up to 1% of any corporation within the Restricted Territory that is listed on a national stock exchange and that is engaged in a business that is competitive with Heritage, HBC or an Affiliated Company, but Shareholder may not otherwise participate (whether in management or otherwise) in such corporation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Restricted Territory' from multiple counties to only Santa Clara and Alameda. However, clause (iv) in both the changed and contradicted texts still refers to actions prohibited 'anywhere in the originally defined Restricted Territory'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the definition of 'Restricted Territory' is inconsistent within the same section of the contract. The change creates uncertainty regarding which geographic area the non-compete applies to, as actions are restricted by the broader original definition, even though the term itself is narrowed.

perturbation.original_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the Shareholder has occupied a position of trust and confidence with Diablo prior to the date hereof and has had access to and has become familiar with the following, any and all of which constitutes confidential information of Diablo (collectively “Confidential Information”) (a) any and all proprietary intellectual property or trade secrets concerning the business and affairs of Diablo, product specifications, data, know-how, formulae, compositions, processes, designs, graphs, drawings, samples, inventions and ideas, past, current and planned research and development, customer lists, current and anticipated customer requirements, price lists, market studies, business plans, computer software and programs (including object code and source code), database technologies, systems, structures, architectures, processes, improvements, devices, know-how, discoveries, concepts, methods, information of Diablo and any other information, however documented, of Diablo that is a trade secret within the meaning of any applicable law; (b) any and all proprietary non-public information concerning the business and affairs of Diablo (which includes any historical financial statements, financial projections, and budgets, historical and projected sales, capital spending budgets and plans, the names and backgrounds of key personnel, contractors, agents, suppliers, personnel training, techniques and materials, manufacturing methods, designs and techniques, purchasing methods and techniques, however documented; and (c) any and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, summaries and other material prepared by or for Diablo containing or based, in whole or part, upon any information included in the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the Shareholder has occupied a position of trust and confidence with Diablo prior to the date hereof and has had access to and has become familiar with the following, any and all of which constitutes confidential information of Diablo (collectively “Confidential Information”) (a) any and all proprietary intellectual property or trade secrets concerning the business and affairs of Diablo, product specifications, data, know-how, formulae, compositions, processes, designs, graphs, drawings, samples, inventions and ideas, past, current and planned research and development, customer lists, current and anticipated customer requirements, price lists, market studies, business plans, computer software and programs (including object code and source code), database technologies, systems, structures, architectures, processes, improvements, devices, know-how, discoveries, concepts, methods, information of Diablo and any other information, however documented, of Diablo that is a trade secret within the meaning of any applicable law; (b) any and all proprietary non-public information concerning the business and affairs of Diablo, financial projections, and budgets, historical and projected sales, capital spending budgets and plans, the names and backgrounds of key personnel, contractors, agents, suppliers, personnel training, techniques and materials, manufacturing methods, designs and techniques, purchasing methods and techniques, however documented; and (c) any and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, summaries and other material prepared by or for Diablo containing or based, in whole or part, upon any information included in the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by removing 'any historical financial statements' from the list of Confidential Information in Section 3(b). While Section 3(b) defines confidential information, Section 3(c) refers to 'any information included in the foregoing'. This creates an inconsistency because it's unclear whether historical financial statements, which were initially part of the definition, are still considered confidential under the scope of 3(c). This uncertainty impacts the enforceability of the confidentiality clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) any and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, summaries and other material prepared by or for Diablo containing or based, in whole or part, upon any information included in the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'any historical financial statements' from the definition of Confidential Information in section 3(b). The contradicted text, section 3(c), refers to 'any information included in the foregoing.' Because the original text defined 'any historical financial statements' as part of the information included in the 'foregoing', and this was then removed in the changed text, Section 3(c) now has a contradiction of whether or not historical financial statements would be considered as being based on the information included in the foregoing.

perturbation.original_text: Therefore, Shareholder hereby agrees not to, at any time, disclose to any unauthorized Persons or use for his or its own account or for the benefit of any third party any Confidential Information, whether or not such information is 2 embodied in writing or other physical form or is retained in the memory of Shareholder, without Heritage’s written consent, unless and to the extent that the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public other than as a result of Shareholder’s fault or the fault of any other Person bound by a duty of confidentiality to Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies.
perturbation.changed_text: Therefore, Shareholder hereby agrees not to, at any time, disclose to any unauthorized Persons or use for his or its own account or for the benefit of any third party any Confidential Information, whether or not such information is 2 embodied in writing or other physical form or is retained in the memory of Shareholder, without Heritage’s written consent, unless and to the extent that the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 3(b) by removing the phrase 'other than as a result of Shareholder’s fault or the fault of any other Person bound by a duty of confidentiality to Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies'. Now, the confidentiality clause lacks the exception regarding the fault of Shareholder or related persons. This implies that Shareholder is not liable if the disclosure happens due to their fault. This is contradictory because it weakens the intended protection and creates a loophole
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the protection of the Confidential Information is necessary to protect and preserve the value of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties being acquired by Heritage and HBC. Therefore, Shareholder hereby agrees not to, at any time, disclose to any unauthorized Persons or use for his or its own account or for the benefit of any third party any Confidential Information, whether or not such information is 2 embodied in writing or other physical form or is retained in the memory of Shareholder, without Heritage’s written consent, unless and to the extent that the Confidential Information is or becomes generally known to and available for use by the public.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an exception to the non-disclosure agreement if the confidential information becomes public due to the fault of the Shareholder or related parties. The changed text removes this exception. The contradicted text emphasizes the importance of protecting Confidential Information. Removing the exception creates a contradiction as the Shareholder is no longer liable if the information becomes public due to their actions, weakening the confidentiality protection the document intends to enforce. This aligns with the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition, where conflicting obligations are introduced.

perturbation.original_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the restrictions set forth in this Section 3 are reasonable in scope and essential to the preservation of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties and that enforcement of these restrictions will not cause Shareholder any hardship, and because of Shareholder’s background and experience, will not in any manner preclude Shareholder from becoming gainfully employed in such a manner and to such an extent as will provide a standard of living for Shareholder and the members of Shareholder’s family of at least the sort and fashion to which they have become accustomed.
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the restrictions set forth in this Section 3 are reasonable in scope and essential to the preservation of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties. Shareholder believes that enforcement of these restrictions will not cause Shareholder any undue hardship.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 3(d) by omitting specific justifications about the Shareholder’s future employment prospects. By changing 'will not cause Shareholder any hardship, and because of Shareholder’s background and experience, will not in any manner preclude Shareholder from becoming gainfully employed in such a manner and to such an extent as will provide a standard of living for Shareholder and the members of Shareholder’s family of at least the sort and fashion to which they have become accustomed' to 'will not cause Shareholder any undue hardship', it creates a discrepancy. By removing the original sentance, Shareholder might not have as much recourse when enforcing against what's deemed and undue hardship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the restrictions set forth in this Section 3 are reasonable in scope and essential to the preservation of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties. Shareholder believes that enforcement of these restrictions will not cause Shareholder any undue hardship. Each of Heritage, HBC and Shareholder acknowledges and agrees that the covenants and agreements contained in this Section 3 have been negotiated in good faith by each of them. Each of Heritage, HBC and Shareholder further acknowledges that (i) the goodwill associated with the existing vendors, customers, assets and employees of Diablo prior to the transactions contemplated herein is an integral component of the value of Diablo to Heritage and HBC and is reflected in the consideration to be received by Diablo shareholders, including the Shareholder pursuant to this Agreement, and (ii) the covenants and agreements contained in this Section 3 are necessary to preserve the value of Diablo’s business and proprietary properties for Heritage and HBC following the transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided specific assurances regarding the Shareholder's future employment prospects and standard of living after the restrictions are enforced. The changed text removes these specific assurances, replacing them with a more general statement about 'undue hardship'. This weakens the Shareholder's position and creates a discrepancy within Section 3. By omitting the original sentence, the change now creates less recourse for the Shareholder when enforcing against what's deemed 'undue hardship' in the Contradicted Text, leading to a contradiction as per the Perturbation Explanation and Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: If a court or arbitrator has determined that Shareholder has committed a breach by Shareholder of any covenant set forth in Section 3 of this Agreement, the Restricted Period will be extended by the period of the duration of such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: If a court or arbitrator has determined that Shareholder has committed a breach of any covenant set forth in Section 3 of this Agreement, the Restricted Period will be extended by a period of one year.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation causes an in-text contradiction within Section 3(c). The original clause states that if a breach is determined, the Restricted Period will extend by the duration of said breach. However, the changed clause states the Restricted Period will be extended by one year no matter the length of the duration of such breach. The changed clause sets a definitive period, while the original clause makes the extension dependent on the duration of the breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder, Heritage and HBC each recognize and acknowledge that the Confidential Information and other knowledge Shareholder has about Diablo and has and will obtain from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies is special and unique, and any violation of the covenants contained in this Agreement is likely to cause irreparable damage to Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies. Therefore, the parties agree that, upon any breach of any covenant contained in this Section 3 by Shareholder, Heritage and HBC shall be entitled to an appropriate injunction for a violation of such covenant, threatened or actual, of such covenant, in addition to all other relief available under applicable law. If a court or arbitrator has determined that Shareholder has committed a breach of any covenant set forth in Section 3 of this Agreement, the Restricted Period will be extended by a period of one year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the extension period of the Restricted Period. Originally, the extension was tied to the duration of the breach, but the modified text fixes the extension to a period of one year, regardless of the breach's duration. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This new, fixed period directly conflicts with the original, variable period, creating a clear inconsistency within Section 3 and a strong in-text contradiction as it removes the dependency on the actual duration of the breach.

perturbation.original_text: Following receipt of such notice, if Heritage in its reasonable judgment determines that Shareholder’s proposed association involves a Person engaged in the Business, Heritage may serve notice upon each such Person that such Shareholder is bound by this Agreement and furnish each such Person with a copy of this Agreement or relevant portions thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Following receipt of such notice, if Heritage determines that Shareholder’s proposed association involves a Person engaged in the Business, Heritage will serve notice upon each such Person that such Shareholder is bound by this Agreement and furnish each such Person with a copy of this Agreement or relevant portions thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the phrase 'in its reasonable judgment' from Section 3(f). Originally, it was stated that 'if Heritage in its reasonable judgment determines'. Now, the revised clause states 'if Heritage determines'. Without 'in its reasonable judgement', this creates an in-text contradiction becuase any judgment made by Heritage is not restricted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder will, during the Restrictive Period, within ten days after accepting any employment, consulting engagement, engagement as an independent contractor, partnership or other association that might reasonably involve the Business, advise Heritage of the identity of the new employer, client, partner or other Person with whom Shareholder has become associated. Following receipt of such notice, if Heritage determines that Shareholder’s proposed association involves a Person engaged in the Business, Heritage will serve notice upon each such Person that such Shareholder is bound by this Agreement and furnish each such Person with a copy of this Agreement or relevant portions thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modification removes 'in its reasonable judgment' from the original text. This creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text because Heritage's determination is now unrestricted, potentially leading to arbitrary decisions that conflict with the shareholder's understanding and expectations as expressed in the contradicted text. The original text's restriction is now lifted, and the judgement is unrestricted.
